stories.headline: Mapping the latest US strikes across Iran
stories.summary: US attacks kill dozens in Iran as Iranian forces target regional bases, pushing a fragile ceasefire closer to collapse.

stories.headline: Russian attacks kill 14 as Ukraine hits Black Sea oil tankers
stories.summary: Civilian casualties are reported in a number of Ukrainian regions, while Kyiv hits 20 Russian vessels in the Black Sea.

stories.headline: US resumes daylight strikes on Iran as tensions escalate over Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: US Central Command said it has resumed daylight strikes on Iran, targeting coastal defences and missile sites. Tehran has reported casualties as tensions rise over a renewed blockade threat, calls for retaliation and the ongoing dispute over the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.headline: Volodymyr Zelenskyy dismisses Ukraine’s popular defence minister
stories.summary: Mykhailo Fedorov, credited with transforming the ministry, ousted in reshuffle after six months in postVolodymyr Zelenskyy has dismissed Ukraine’s popular defence minister, despite pleas from foreign partners and civil society for him to keep his job, as part of wide-ranging government reshuffle.In a post on Telegram Mykhailo Fedorov announced that he was leaving his position, saying it had been a “great honour” to serve the Ukrainian people. He

stories.headline: Zelenskyy dismisses Ukraine’s popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov
stories.summary: “It was a great honour to serve the Ukrainian people as defence minister," Fedorov said on Wednesday evening, confirming his dismissal amid Zelenskyy’s government reshuffle. While not a very public figure, Fedorov has been widely praised within the military and among civil society.

stories.headline: Protests in Paris, Gaza for release of Hussam Abu Safia
stories.summary: Protests were held in Paris and Gaza demanding the release of Palestinian doctor Hussam Abu Safia.

stories.headline: Iran threatens to block more trade routes as US launches fresh strikes
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump vowed to strike Iran's bridges and power plants next week if the country does not return to talks.

stories.headline: Blanche grilled over loyalty to Trump in tense confirmation hearing
stories.summary: Acting attorney-general vows to resign if asked to act illegally but insists he serves at pleasure of the president

stories.headline: U.S. Military Forces Again Blockade Iranian Ports
stories.summary: Following the collapse of the cease-fire with Iran, President Trump ordered U.S. warships and aircraft to stop vessels going to and from Iran.

stories.headline: Almost Half of House Democrats Vote to End Aid to Israel
stories.summary: The measure failed, but the level of support among Democrats exposed a stark shift in the party away from backing the Jewish state.

stories.headline: Venezuela’s interim government and opposition to begin formal talks
stories.summary: Nobel laureate María Corina Machado will not lead negotiations over new elections, contrary to expectationsThe interim government of Venezuela has announced it will begin formal talks with the opposition aimed at “strengthening democracy” in the country.The move is backed by the US, which says it is seeking a “democratic transition” in a country still recovering from the twin earthquakes that killed more than 4,700 people. stories.headline: Todd Blanche’s loyalty to Trump questioned in Senate confirmation hearing
stories.summary: Blanche's actions have raised questions about the independence of the Department of Justice, which he currently leads.

stories.headline: French parliament passes assisted dying bill for some terminally ill adults
stories.summary: After years of debate, French MPs have voted to adopt a bill to establish a right to assisted dying for some adults suffering from an incurable condition.

stories.headline: Ukraine: Six dead as Russia targets Sumy and Odesa
stories.summary: Emergency crews battled fires and searched damaged buildings across Ukraine on Tuesday 15 July after Russian strikes on the cities of Sumy and Odesa killed six people and injured at least 20 others, according to regional authorities.

stories.headline: French MPs approve assisted dying law with strict rules after years of argument
stories.summary: The bill would allow assisted dying for terminally ill adults who meet strict criteria.

stories.headline: Italy's Meloni suffers surprise setback in close vote on electoral reform
stories.summary: The defeat triggered opposition calls for Meloni to resign ahead of next year's general election.

stories.headline: Venezuela government to launch formal talks with opposition members
stories.summary: The opposition says the aim of the talks is to strengthen the democratic institutions and the electoral system.

stories.headline: China economic growth falls sharply, missing target
stories.summary: Weak demand domestically and the impact of the Iran war on oil prices overshadowed the country's strong exports.

stories.headline: Sierra Leone drops treason charge against ex-president over attempted coup
stories.summary: Ernest Bai Koroma was arrested in 2023 after gunmen broke into an armoury and some jails, freeing inmates.

stories.headline: China detains US scientist who studied North Korea nuclear tests
stories.summary: Chen Youlin's family says he has been wrongfully detained for nearly two years on spying charges.

stories.headline: Oil traders warn market is close to running on empty as Hormuz shuts again
stories.summary: Stockpiles that acted as shock absorbers early in Iran war are running low as crucial waterway closes again

stories.headline: JPMorgan Leads $3 Billion Financing for Warburg’s Pantherx Buy
stories.summary: A group of lenders led by JPMorgan Chase & Co. is lining up a debt package worth up to $3 billion to support Warburg Pincus’ acquisition of Pantherx Specialty LLC, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Short-Sellers Swarm SpaceX With Paper Profit Nearing $4 Billion
stories.summary: SpaceX bears are minting billions on paper as the company’s stock price cracks ahead of a closely-watched rocket launch and upcoming earnings that will unlock a flood of shares.

stories.headline: Saudi Gulf Oil Loads Slump as Iran Hits Tankers in Hormuz
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s oil loadings from inside the Persian Gulf have slumped in the wake of an upsurge of attacks on supertankers in the Strait of Hormuz, satellite images reviewed by Bloomberg show, demonstrating how Iran is once again disrupting supply.

stories.headline: House G.O.P. Releases Budget to Unlock $95 Billion for Iran War and SAVE Act
stories.summary: The plan faces a rocky path amid Republican divisions and would pave the way for votes months before the midterm elections to fund a war that polls show is deeply unpopular.

stories.headline: What to Know About Todd Blanche’s Order Granting Trump Immunity From I.R.S. Audits
stories.summary: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche’s decision to grant President Trump protection from I.R.S. audits was a central issue in his confirmation hearing on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Biden Will Release Memoir Just After the November Midterms
stories.summary: The former president announced that the book, “Promise Me, America,” will come out on Nov. 17, and will cover the tumultuous events of his presidency as well as his re-election bid.

stories.headline: Senator Mark Kelly Amasses Nearly $25 Million Campaign War Chest
stories.summary: The Arizona Democrat said he had raised and given away $10 million to fellow members of his party and committees as he mulls a presidential run in 2028.

stories.headline: Germany warns US against election interference after it announces grants scheme

stories.headline: French Parliament approves landmark assisted-dying bill

stories.headline: ICE should continue traffic stops after shootings, Trump says, apparently contradicting new policy

stories.headline: Former President Joe Biden to publish memoir after November US midterm elections
stories.summary: The memoir will be released two weeks after the US midterm elections, a timing that could spark debate among Democrats, who remain divided over Biden's legacy and his decision to seek a second White House term.

stories.headline: Rastafarians in Kenya fail in bid to get cannabis legalised for religious purposes

stories.summary: Your flight? Canceled. The silver lining? You might be able to make some money off the misery.

stories.headline: Brazil to Offer Billions in Debt Relief for Struggling Farmers
stories.summary: Brazil’s government plans to publish a provisional measure on Wednesday to renegotiate more than 100 billion reais ($20 billion) in rural debt, as President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva prepares to seek reelection in October.

stories.headline: SpaceX IPO Lifts Wall Street ECM Revenue to Best Since 2021
stories.summary: Wall Street’s biggest investment banks hauled in the most revenue from advising on equity offerings in the second quarter since 2021, fueled by SpaceX’s record-setting IPO and a fundraising blitz for artificial intelligence infrastructure.

stories.summary: Smaller deals involving regional banks and wealth managers are driving acquisitions in the financial services industry, according to a clutch of industry watchers.

stories.headline: British couple return to village at heart of deadly Spanish wildfire
stories.summary: At least 13 people, including five believed to be Britons, were killed by Thursday's wildfire in Spain's Almeria province.

stories.headline: Russian anti-war politician Boris Nadezhdin detained by police
stories.summary: Boris Nadezhdin attempted to run against Vladimir Putin at the 2024 presidential election, but was prevented from doing so.

stories.headline: German court convicts Iraqi couple of enslaving Yazidi girls
stories.summary: The pair, named only as Twana H.S. and Asia R. A., left Germany for Iraq in 2015 and became members of the Islamic State group.

stories.headline: Hungary parliament votes to remove president from office

stories.headline: Former Emir of Qatar Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani dies aged 74
stories.summary: He took power in 1996 after staging a bloodless coup against his father, and went on to transform Qatar into the gas-rich power it is today.
